{{Quote|In the right place, your heart was. But clouded by emotion was your judgment.|[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]|<ref name="aow" />}}
'''Soon Bayts''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. When the [[Yinchorri Uprising]] came to bear, Bayts was charged with supervising communications between the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] strike teams and the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. When the temple was attacked by [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] raiders during the Uprising, Bayts fought alongside his fellow Jedi to defend his home.

By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Bayts was a Master, and he joined [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] in accompanying [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] on the ''[[Intervention]]''. Following a rugged crash landing on [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]], Master Bayts was among the advance group scouting for [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] installations on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Upon reaching the base, they were met with heavy [[Droid/Legends|droid]] resistance, as well as the presence of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. Before Bayts even had a chance to attack Grievous, the droid general swiftly killed the Jedi Master by crushing his skull.

==Biography==
===Yinchorri Uprising===
During the [[Yinchorri Uprising]] of [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Soon Bayts was tasked with keeping track of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] strike teams sent to the [[Yinchorri system]]. When the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was [[Raid on the Jedi Temple (Yinchorri Uprising)|attacked]], Bayts fought alongside the other Jedi to defend it from the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] threat. Following the battle, he received word from [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]] [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], determining that Yinchorri command was not located on [[Yitheeth]] as had been suspected. He also learned that [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] strike team had not engaged the opposition, and was lying in wait on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]]. Delivering this news to [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], he encountered the [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] interrogating a Yinchorri survivor of the battle.<ref name="aow" />

[[File:BaytsOff.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Jedi Master Bayts leaps to Yoda's defense.]]
Bayts was curious as to how Yoda had made the prisoner so fearful, but the Grand Master explained that the warrior had brought the fear with him when he attacked the temple, and that he had simply shown him the reality of it. While Bayts delivered his message, the warrior made to attack Yoda, inciting the younger Jedi to leap to the Grand Master's aid. Yoda, however, simply levitated Bayts as he deflected the warrior's attack, and shoved the warrior into the wall, incapacitating him. Yoda then reprimanded Bayts, telling him that emotion had clouded his judgment.<ref name="aow" />

Shortly thereafter, Bayts received further reports from the Yinchorri system: All three strike teams had rendezvoused on Yinchorr, and a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] prisoner had been taken. Upon delivering the news to Yoda, Bayts speculated that the Devaronians might have been behind the mysterious uprising. Later, Master Windu called with urgent news about the Yinchorri command center's location, and Bayts once again had to rush into Yoda's presence, alerting him to the situation. The uprising was eventually quelled thanks to the efforts of the strike teams, though three Jedi perished in the conflict.<ref name="aow" />

===Death at the Battle of Boz Pity===
By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Soon Bayts had ascended to the rank of Jedi Master. For unknown reasons, he changed the color of his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] blade from orange to blue. Along with Mace Windu and several of the Jedi, among them veterans of the Yinchorri Uprising, he accompanied Senator [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] on the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Intervention]]'', bound for the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Alderaan had many ties to the Outer Rim worlds, and when Organa learned that the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] was retreating there, he felt the need to witness the battles firsthand.<ref name="obb" />

[[File:TheDeathofSoonBaytes.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Soon Bayts is killed by Grievous on Boz Pity.]]
Based on intelligence from Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], the ''Intervention'' journeyed to [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]], where a large Confederate blockade was known to be amassing. Since they were but one ship against an entire flotilla, the Jedi enlisted [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], to help pilot the capital ship through the blockade, resulting in a crash landing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface. Master Bayts ventured out with Senator Organa and a detachment of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]].<ref name="obb" />

The group met heavy resistance at the Confederate base, [[Battle of Boz Pity (Clone Wars)|fighting]] off hordes of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. Organa called for a charge, and Bayts obliged. As Bayts cut his way through to the opening of the base, he encountered the [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. Before Bayts could even attack the general, Grievous grasped his head with one of his mechanical claws, and crushed it, killing the Jedi Master instantly. The General then took Bayts' lightsaber, and used it to attack [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] troops. The Battle of Boz Pity was eventually won by the Republic, however.<ref name="obb" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:SoonBaytslightsaber.png|thumb|Soon Bayts's lightsaber which had an orange blade but later changed to blue]]
Soon Bayts was a prompt individual, and always made sure that his superiors were well informed with the intelligence he gleaned as supervisor of communications during the Yinchorri uprising. He was able to survive the Yinchorri attack, and readily went to Master Yoda's aid, even when he did not need it. He acted on emotion, rather than instinct; a trait that Yoda openly criticized. Though Bayts' combat skills served him well during the Yinchorri conflict, they did not match those of General Grievous, who killed the Jedi Master without difficulty; taking his blue lightsaber as a trophy.<ref name="obb" /><ref name="aow" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|When the character Soon Baytes was first introduced, in ''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' (2000), someone else was editing the comics…|[[Sue Rostoni]]|<ref name="swf">{{SWBoards|url=message.jspa?messageID=10042225#10042225|thread=Welcome some BCaT VIPs|user=[[Sue Rostoni]]|date=2005-03-25|time=10:39 AM}}</ref>}}
Soon Bayts was created by [[Randy Stradley]], and first appeared in the comic mini-series [[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'']]. In it, the character appears in a minor supporting role. Bayts' name was intended as a pun on the term "masturbates"; a pun later illustrated by [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]] in ''[[Star Wars: Obsession]]''. Stradley had initially planned to use the name as a practical joke on Comic Editor at Lucas Licensing [[Sue Rostoni]], who would often put the word "Master" in front of any Jedi's name. Stradley created Bayts as an unimportant tertiary character simply to play a joke on Rostoni, but the character made it through to publication.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/28559271|board=Literature|thread=Funny SW Lit names|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2008-06-08|time=7:34 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200604155614/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/funny-sw-lit-names.28317507/page-2}}</ref>

Following Blackman's use of the character, Comic Editor at Lucas Licensing [[Sue Rostoni]] would publicly lament that she was not in charge of editing at the time of Bayts' creation.<ref name="swf" /> In ''Star Wars: Obsession'', Bayts is once again used as a minor supporting character. Perhaps due to the nature of Soon Bayts' name, references to him outside ''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' and ''Star Wars: Obsession'' have been virtually non-existent, save for the mention of him in [[Abel G. Peña]]'s ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article, ''[[Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous]]'', and an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.

Bayts name was spelled in two different ways in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], both "Bayts" and "Baytes." In ''Jedi Council: Acts of War'', his name is only mentioned once, and it is spelled "Bayts." In ''Star Wars: Obsession'', it is also spelled in this manner, save for one panel. But on the same page, the original spelling is used as well.<ref name="obb" /><ref name="aow" /> Abel G. Peña, the most recent author to reference the character, uses the spelling "Bayts", but alludes to a pronunciation closer to "bey-tiz", to avoid the former implications<ref name="us">{{InsiderCite|86|Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous}}</ref>.
